profile.headline: Full-stack Software Engineer
profile.bio:
I’m a software developer with over 3 years of commercial experience, currently looking to transition into backend-focused web development.

In my professional work, I’ve primarily developed in C#, contributing to production-ready projects. My experience includes working with system-level concerns, memory management, and performance optimization - including GPU-based parallel processing in critical areas. This background has given me a strong foundation in writing efficient, scalable code.

Alongside this, I’ve been actively expanding into web technologies. I work with .NET Core, ASP.NET, and have hands-on experience with databases and backend tooling such as Redis, SQL (including MySQL and PostgreSQL). On the frontend side, I’ve worked with Vue.js, Svelte, and modern tooling like Vite, as well as a range of other technologies I’ve explored as part of personal and professional growth.

You can also find a recommendation on my LinkedIn profile that reflects my broader technical skillset.

I’m interested in joining a team where I can apply my existing experience while continuing to grow as a backend developer, and contribute to building reliable, high-quality systems.

projects.title: Real-time Room Reverb Simulation
projects.summary: A Unity implementation of a research paper on geometry-aware real-time reverb via transport-path precomputation.
projects.description:
A Unity implementation of the technique from the paper "Real-time Room Reverb in Large Scenes using Transport Path Precomputation" by Gregor Mückl and Carsten Dachsbacher (JCGT, 2020).

The method makes reverb geometry-aware: instead of faking room acoustics, it models how sound actually propagates through a scene. The pipeline samples surface points across scene geometry, then precomputes sound-transport paths between them so the acoustic response can be evaluated in real time even in large environments.

Implemented stages so far: surface-point generation across mesh geometry, and transport-path precomputation for sound propagation — driven on the GPU with compute shaders.

technologies.name: C#
technologies.note: Surface-point generation and transport-path precomputation stages.

technologies.name: HLSL / Compute Shaders
technologies.note: GPU compute shaders drive the precomputation.

technologies.name: Unity
technologies.note: Scene tooling and runtime host for the simulation.

images.caption: Research paper the project is based on.
An example of a transport path.

projects.title: Boids 2D - Flocking Simulation
projects.summary: A Unity flocking simulation of birds/fish from three simple rules, with an interactive control UI.
projects.description:
A 2D Unity simulation of the flocking behaviour seen in flocks of birds and shoals of fish. The emergent group motion comes from three simple per-agent rules: separation (avoid crowding neighbours), cohesion (steer toward the local group centre), and alignment (match neighbours' heading).

An interactive UI lets you tune how strongly each of the three factors influences movement in real time, and a debug view visualises every boid's vision radius and the individual steering contribution of each factor.

technologies.name: C#
technologies.note: Separation, cohesion and alignment steering with tunable weights.

technologies.name: Unity
technologies.note: 2D simulation with a debug view of each boid's vision radius.

projects.title: Flashcard Generator
projects.summary: A Python CLI that turns JSON config + live API data into print-ready flashcard PDFs.
projects.description:
A command-line tool for generating print-ready flashcard PDFs entirely from JSON configuration — no code required to create a new set.

The architecture cleanly separates two phases. The generate phase fetches data from external APIs, resolves field values, and builds card data structures into an intermediate flashcards-input.json. The render phase then merges that data with layout templates, applies per-card overrides, and renders to PDF via ReportLab and Cairo (pycairo / rlPyCairo).

Built as an installable package exposing an `fcpdf` command (Click), with an interactive prompt-driven UX — new / generate / render / preview. Supports custom TrueType fonts (freetype-py), QR codes, and on-the-fly translation (deep-translator). Covered by a pytest suite.

Private repository — source and run instructions are not public.

technologies.name: Click
technologies.note: Interactive fcpdf CLI — new / generate / render / preview commands.

technologies.name: ReportLab
technologies.note: Print-ready PDF layout and output.

technologies.name: Python
technologies.note: Two-phase pipeline: API data generation, then template-driven rendering.

technologies.name: Pillow
technologies.note: Card image processing.

technologies.name: pycairo
technologies.note: Cairo rendering backend (rlPyCairo) for crisp vector output.

images.caption: An example of a printed flashcard set.

projects.title: CS2 Trader Ultimate
projects.summary: A self-hosted full-stack tool for tracking CS2 skin prices and discovering profitable trade-up contracts.
projects.description:
A self-hosted web app for trading CS2 (Counter-Strike 2) skins. It tracks market prices across every wear condition and StatTrak variant, charts price history over time, surfaces profitable trade-up contracts filtered by risk, profitability, and rarity, and maps which contracts the owned inventory is eligible for.

The backend is a type-safe Bun + Elysia API; an Eden Treaty client carries end-to-end types straight into the Vue 3 + Vite frontend. Redis — with its JSON, Search, and TimeSeries modules — stores skins, price series, and pre-computed trade-up data, while BullMQ runs background scraping jobs. The whole stack is orchestrated with Docker Compose.

Private repository — source and run instructions are not public.

technologies.name: TypeScript
technologies.note: End-to-end type safety via an Eden Treaty client generated from the API.
